summary: Leading private tertiary-care hospital group in Nigeria, the first in Sub-Saharan Africa to be JCI-accredited and reaccredited, now backed by Iwosan Investments.
offering: Tertiary-level private clinical care across multiple specialties, positioned around international-quality standards.

pricing.model: Private-pay and insurance, premium tertiary-care positioning
pricing.notes: No published price list; consultation/procedure-based billing typical of premium private hospitals.

positioning: The leading private tertiary-care organization in Nigeria, benchmarked against international quality standards (JCI accreditation).
strengths: First hospital group in Sub-Saharan Africa to be JCI-accredited and reaccredited, a strong international quality signal
Backed by Iwosan Investments (2021 acquisition), providing capital for continued expansion
Multiple locations across key Lagos catchments (Ikoyi, Ikeja, Victoria Island)
gaps_or_weaknesses: Premium positioning may price out cost-sensitive patient segments
Concentrated in Lagos, with limited footprint outside the city/region
perceived_target_customer: Higher-income private-pay and insured patients in Lagos seeking internationally benchmarked tertiary care.
confidence_notes: Ownership and accreditation history sourced from secondary press coverage (Nairametrics); no direct pricing or patient-volume data available publicly.
